title: Study of RAS(ON) Inhibitors in Patients With Advanced RAS-mutated NSCLC

eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures eligibility for the trial is within a defined age range, which is a feasibility consideration.
This criterion limits patients with severe illness or functional impairment, ensuring their safety and well-being.

eligibility.text: Adequate organ function as outlined by the study
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient's ability to tolerate treatment and participate in the trial, which is a feasibility consideration.

eligibility.text: Received prior standard therapy appropriate for tumor type and stage
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have received adequate pre-treatment care to minimize potential adverse events or complications, which is a safety consideration.

eligibility.text: Must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRAS G12C-mutated solid tumor malignancy (not amenable to curative surgery)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogeneous and allows for a comparison of treatment effects on this specific patient group.

eligibility.text: Must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic RAS-mutated NSCLC
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogeneous and allows for a comparison of treatment effects on this specific patient group.

eligibility.text: Must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic RAS G12D-mutated NSCLC
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogeneous and allows for a comparison of treatment effects on this specific patient group.
This criterion limits patients with potential neurological complications, ensuring their safety and well-being.

eligibility.text: Impaired gastrointestinal (GI) function that may significantly alter the absorption of RMC drugs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have adequate digestive function to absorb the medication, which is a feasibility consideration.

eligibility.text: Major surgery < 28 days of first dose
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures sufficient time for recovery from surgery before starting treatment, which is a feasibility consideration.

eligibility.text: Active or history of interstitial lung disease (ILD) or pneumonitis requiring steroids
eligibility.justification: This criterion limits patients with potential respiratory complications, ensuring their safety and well-being.
This criterion allows for flexibility in the trial design to accommodate specific patient populations or research needs, which is a feasibility consideration.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

known_effects: Treatment response - KRAS G12D mutation required

pharmacogenetics.genetic_screen_failure_summary: Detected 2 genetic biomarker requirement(s) in eligibility: KRAS_G12C (pop. prevalence ~13%, ~87% screen fail); KRAS_G12D (pop. prevalence ~5%, ~95% screen fail). Worst-case screen failure rate from genetic criteria: ~95%.
pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (KRAS, NRAS, HRAS, PD1, PDL1, CTLA4) but could not map indication "non-small cell lung cancer" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.

relaxations.description: Decrease minimum age from 18 to 13 years
